title: Soul Is an Artifact, Not a Substance — Daniel Fox
meta_description: A machine wrote a song that made me cry. What we call soul is a set of learnable artifacts, and admitting that is harder on the ego than on the music.
schema: <script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Article","headline":"Soul Is an Artifact, Not a Substance","description":"What we call soul is a set of learnable artifacts that signal vulnerability, and admitting it costs the ego more than the music.","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox","alternateName":"Daniel Fox","jobTitle":"Retail Music Behaviorist","url":"https://danielchristopherfox.com"},"publisher":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox"},"mainEntityOfPage":"https://danielchristopherfox.com/essays/what-we-call-soul.html","about":["AI music","Creativity","Music","Authenticity"]}</script>
